citations.title: Google Gemini can now control your computer — and why that matters
MCP vs RAG vs Skills: AI agent architecture

design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed=hash('Text message AI assistant|use_case|people who want to text an AI assistant instead of opening another app|2026-07-06T08:35:26.940634+00:00') → selected architecture=workflow-command-center; AIDA Check: hero=Attention, proof+bento=Interest, motion+examples=Desire, CTA band=Action; Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk; Hero Math Verification: max-width 1120px clamp(44px–72px) ensures 2–3 lines; Bento Density Verification: grid-auto-flow:dense present; GSAP Motion Selection: image-scale-fade-scroll + hover-physics; Label Sweep & Button Check: black primary CTA, white secondary CTA verified
headline: Text message AI assistant that actually does the work

site_brief.audience: people who want to text an AI assistant instead of opening another app
site_brief.goal: Create an indexable SEO use-case page in the /use-cases/ cluster. Target the practical query 'Text message AI assistant', show the exact job-to-be-done, and explain how Super can perform the workflow with real computer use and a reusable computer-use cache. Include sibling use-case links so the cluster feels intentional, crawlable, and useful. Add a long-form operator playbook with how-to steps, setup checklist, realistic limits, confirmation moments, and examples that a reader can act on immediately.
site_brief.title: Text message AI assistant
site_brief.use_case_intent: show how Super turns SMS-style requests into real computer actions, follow-ups, reminders, and app workflows

summary: A practical, operator‑grade guide to using Super as a text message AI assistant — turning simple SMS-style requests into real computer actions, repeatable workflows, and confirmations, powered by a reusable computer-use cache.
